packageRules.description: Runtime dependencies ship in the published wheel, so their bumps should cut a release. Everything else Renovate touches (dev groups, GitHub Actions, the uv build stage) defaults to a non-releasable 'chore'.

The runtime base image ships in the published container, which is built only on release tags — so a base-image bump must cut a release to reach the published image. The uv builder stage is build-only and stays a non-releasable 'chore'.

astral-sh/uv releases faster than the 7-day stability gate, so the newest tag is always <7 days old and the base-image FROM update never matures out of 'pending' — freezing us several releases behind. Relax the soak for this one trusted, digest-pinned first-party image only; the 7-day default still protects every other dependency.

lockFileMaintenance.description: Renovate's pep621 manager only re-locks when a pyproject.toml constraint changes, so versions that already satisfy our ranges (transitive deps especially) freeze in uv.lock between constraint bumps. Lock file maintenance runs a periodic full re-resolve (the automated equivalent of 'uv lock --upgrade') and opens a PR, gated by the existing 'uv lock --check' + pytest matrix. The runtime image is built with 'uv sync --frozen', so uv.lock is exactly what ships in the published container — like a base-image bump, a lock refresh must cut a release to reach it, so these updates are a releasable 'deps'.
